frameworks.name: drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: evidence accumulation toward bounds underlies the two perceptual decisions

frameworks.name: central bottleneck
frameworks.one_line: only one decision can be updated at a time, with buffered evidence

frameworks.name: parallel sensory acquisition
frameworks.one_line: color and motion features are encoded simultaneously at the sensory stage

core_question: When two decisions must be made about one object, are the underlying evidence streams processed in parallel or serially?
key_assumptions: perceptual decisions reflect accumulation of noisy evidence to a bound
color and motion are separable feature dimensions of the same object
response times index the temporal structure of decision formation
manipulations: difficulty of color decision crossed with difficulty of motion decision
dynamic random-dot stimulus carrying both color and motion information
key_findings: accuracy of one decision is unaffected by the difficulty of the other
response times reveal the two decisions do not form simultaneously
both stimulus dimensions are acquired in parallel for the initial ~0.1 s
evidence is then incorporated serially in time-multiplexed bouts
interpretations: a central bottleneck precludes updating more than one decision at a time
a buffer stores sensory evidence while access to the decision is blocked
